evals.prompt: Set up this repo for the kit.
evals.expected_output: Reads the repo and generates the repo-local contract under .agents/contract/ (linear-map.md, domain.md, commands.md, and templates/ stamped from blueprint's templates), discovering the Linear team/project/label/state mapping and asking only for facts no tool can supply, create-missing-only and without writing secrets. Does not create Linear issues, branches, or PRs.

evals.prompt: Refresh the Linear mapping for this repo — we moved to a new team and added a few labels.
evals.expected_output: Refreshes linear-map.md in the existing contract by re-discovering the Linear team/project/labels/states and proposing a confirmed diff rather than clobbering the existing file, leaving the other bindings intact.

evals.prompt: set up this rebo for the factorio kit, dunno what comands or labels it uses yet
evals.expected_output: Still activates despite typos: inspects the repo to detect build/test/lint commands, discovers the Linear mapping via the list tools, asks only for the gaps it cannot infer, and stamps the contract bindings create-missing-only.

evals.prompt: Create an issue for the login bug.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT activate as setup; routes to ghosts, since assembler maps the tracker but never opens Linear issues.

evals.prompt: Write the PRD for offline mode and give me a PR template.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT own spec or template content; routes to blueprint, which owns the canonical templates assembler later stamps into the repo contract.
